records.ety: [L. decentia, fr. decens: cf. F. dcence. See Decent.]

defs: The quality or state of being decent, suitable, or becoming, in words or behavior; propriety of form in social intercourse, in actions, or in discourse; proper formality; becoming ceremony; seemliness; hence, freedom from obscenity or indecorum; modesty.
quotes: Observances of time, place, and of decency in general.
Immodest words admit of no defense, For want of decency is want of sense.

defs: That which is proper or becoming.
quotes: Those thousand decencies, that daily flow From all her words and actions.
